allowed_contents: README, CONTRIBUTING, top-level governance docs, minimal bootstrap entrypoints, module folders, governed shared folders
forbidden_contents: ad hoc logs, run outputs, maintenance csv dumps, transient probes, new scientific report files
source_of_truth_rule: Root is an index layer, not a working dump.
notes: Keep existing historical root files in place until transitioned.

allowed_contents: durable policies, architecture docs, contracts, governance docs, human-readable reference material
forbidden_contents: run outputs, transient logs, bulk figures, live scripts
source_of_truth_rule: Human-readable source of truth for repository policy lives here.
notes: Prefer docs/governance and docs/modules subtrees going forward.

allowed_contents: directory policy, naming rules, source-of-truth rules, lineage policy
forbidden_contents: scientific outputs, code snapshots
source_of_truth_rule: Repository governance documents live here.
notes: Can start as a documentation-only phase.

allowed_contents: module-specific durable architecture notes, contracts, measurement definitions
forbidden_contents: run outputs and figures unless intentionally embedded as documentation examples
source_of_truth_rule: Module docs here are durable cross-run references, not run narratives.
notes: Use only when module-local docs inside source folders are not tightly coupled to code.

allowed_contents: templates, document scaffolds, examples
forbidden_contents: live execution outputs
source_of_truth_rule: Templates are reusable inputs, not outputs.
notes: If executable templates remain, document why.

allowed_contents: reusable utilities, validators, maintenance helpers, figure infrastructure, wrappers
forbidden_contents: durable scientific reports and ad hoc transient outputs
source_of_truth_rule: Reusable infrastructure belongs here.
notes: Distinguish tools/ from scripts/ by reusability.

allowed_contents: repo maintenance helpers and validators
forbidden_contents: maintenance result files
source_of_truth_rule: Maintenance code goes here; maintenance outputs go to reports/maintenance or tables/maintenance_*.csv.
notes: Good fit for existing maintenance scripts.

allowed_contents: documented vendored third-party helpers or assets
forbidden_contents: first-party scientific outputs
source_of_truth_rule: Third-party material must be clearly isolated and documented.
notes: Future-only path; do not move github_repo yet without checks.

allowed_contents: stable top-level orchestrators and human-invoked wrappers
forbidden_contents: generated logs, ad hoc csv files, durable reports
source_of_truth_rule: scripts/ holds live entrypoints, not artifacts.
notes: Optional layer only if consistently used.

allowed_contents: module-scoped stable entrypoints
forbidden_contents: durable artifacts and transient output accumulation
source_of_truth_rule: Live orchestrators should be discoverable by module.
notes: Use this when root run_*.m files are eventually reassigned.

proposed_path: Aging/ , Switching/ , Relaxation ver3/ , MT ver2/
allowed_contents: module-owned source code, helpers, tests, and tightly coupled local docs
forbidden_contents: silent accumulation of generated artifacts, logs, promoted figures, durable csv exports
source_of_truth_rule: Active module folders are source-first and should stay clean.
notes: Do not rename yet; use alias rules for artifact layers.

allowed_contents: run-scoped outputs, manifests, logs, raw execution products, status, checkpoints
forbidden_contents: durable canonical reports, tables, and figures that should be promoted
source_of_truth_rule: results/ is the transient lineage layer.
notes: Keep per-module subtrees.

allowed_contents: manifest.json, entrypoint snapshot, stdout/stderr, raw outputs, temporary figures, audit traces
forbidden_contents: live source under active development, promoted canonical tables or reports
source_of_truth_rule: Every run gets a container; snapshots are immutable lineage evidence.
notes: Do not collapse distinct switching canonical families.

allowed_contents: maintenance execution logs, repo-health manifests, cleanup dry-run outputs
forbidden_contents: scientific module outputs
source_of_truth_rule: Maintenance runs should be segregated from science runs.
notes: Future path; keeps root and tmp cleaner.

allowed_contents: canonical csv summaries, indexes, inventories, durable status tables
forbidden_contents: helper code except explicitly frozen lineage snapshots
source_of_truth_rule: Durable structured exports live here.
notes: Use maintenance_* naming for shared governance tables.

allowed_contents: maintenance audit tables and governance inventories
forbidden_contents: science run data that belongs to module tables
source_of_truth_rule: Maintenance tables are durable repo-health outputs.
notes: Matches this audit task output pattern.

allowed_contents: durable markdown scientific narratives and audit reports
forbidden_contents: execution scripts and raw logs
source_of_truth_rule: Reports are promoted narrative outputs.
notes: Maintenance reports belong in reports/maintenance/.

allowed_contents: repo-health reports, audit markdown, maintenance status packs
forbidden_contents: run logs except brief linked summaries
source_of_truth_rule: Repository maintenance narratives live here.
notes: Already partly established; should become exclusive for maintenance reports.

allowed_contents: promoted exported figures intended for durable reference
forbidden_contents: raw exploratory dumps with no lineage manifest
source_of_truth_rule: Durable figures are promoted here from runs after lineage checks.
notes: Prefer figures/aging over results/aging/figures for future durable outputs.

allowed_contents: historical legacy outputs kept for lineage and recovery
forbidden_contents: new writes
source_of_truth_rule: These are write-closed quarantine areas, not active destinations.
notes: Keep until lineage and code-reference checks justify selective migration.

allowed_contents: explicitly quarantined or superseded material
forbidden_contents: new primary work
source_of_truth_rule: Legacy holding zones must stay clearly marked.
notes: Do not multiply new legacy namespace names.
